event: UFC Fight Night: Allen vs. Costa

items.fight_news: Arnold Allen looks to regain his top-tier momentum by relying on his high-volume boxing to disrupt Melquizael Costa's six-fight win streak.
The central analytical question is whether Costa's explosive kicking range can find a home against the disciplined, southpaw movement of Allen.
The Korean Superboy returns from a long layoff to face a dangerous pressure fighter in Daniel Santos: a classic 'Precision vs. Power' matchup.
Analysis suggests Choi's world-class counter-punching will be the primary barrier for Santos' aggressive, forward-moving style.
Malcolm Wellmaker aims to introduce newcomer Juan Diaz to the big stage by testing his grappling endurance over three rounds.
Stylistically, this fight hinges on whether Diaz's 15-1 regional record can translate to the elite takedown defense required in the UFC.
Bukauskas brings a massive experience edge into this catchweight bout, facing an explosive short-notice replacement in Christian Edwards.
The tactical read here is Bukauskas' ability to use outside kicks to keep the younger, powerful Edwards from closing the distance.
Two bantamweights with high finish rates meet in a bout that oddsmakers expect to stay standing for as long as it lasts.
Analytical models favor Sopaj's body-striking variety to challenge Cuamba's traditionally high-guard defensive shell.
Khaos Williams is hunting for another highlight-reel knockout, but he faces a very technical boxer in Veretennikov who rarely leaves openings.
This fight will likely be decided by whether Veretennikov can survive the initial two-minute storm to take over with technical volume.
A light-heavyweight power struggle where one clean shot could end it: Erslan's early-round explosiveness vs. Tokkos' durability.
The narrative focus is on Erslan's power, but the analytical edge lies in Tokkos' ability to extend the fight into the fatigue window.
Gantt faces an undefeated debutant in Minev, making this a classic veteran test for a highly touted prospect.
Expect Gantt to push a grueling grappling pace to see if the late replacement, Minev, has the conditioning for a high-altitude APEX battle.
Ketlen Vieira needs a dominant wrestling performance to stay in the title conversation against the surging Jacqueline Cavalcanti.
The tactical battle is clear: Vieira wants to clinch and grind, while Cavalcanti must use her superior reach to keep the fight at range.
A middleweight clash between two decorated wrestlers who often find themselves in highlight-reel finishes.
While both are grapplers, analysis points to Petroski's more refined submission game being the differentiator in a scramble-heavy fight.
Polyana Viana looks to secure another lightning-fast submission against the gritty Romanian prospect Alice Ardelean.
Early reads indicate Ardelean's defensive posture on the ground will be her most critical asset against Viana's dangerous guard.
Flyweight speed is on full display as the veteran Barez welcomes the technical Gurule to the Octagon.
The matchup data suggests Barez will try to force a high-chaos brawl to disrupt Gurule's more methodical striking approach.
Ireland's Bannon opens the card with a volume-based strategy against the heavy-handed Brazilian Caliari.
The outcome depends on whether Bannon's footwork can successfully evade the pocket-striking power that Caliari is known for.
